chunks: The Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) is the National Standards Body of India working under the Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution, Government of India. It is established by the Bureau of Indian Standards Act 2016. Core functions include Standards Formulation, Product Certification, Hallmarking of Gold and Silver Jewellery, L

aboratory Services, Training Services and Consumer Affairs. BIS promotes standardization, marking and quality certification of goods. BIS has regional offices across India and operates through its head office in New Delhi.

BIS Product Certification scheme certifies that a product conforms to a specific Indian Standard. The ISI Mark is the most popular certification mark in India. Products can be certified voluntarily or mandatorily. Mandatory certification covers products affecting health and safety such as electrical appliances, cement, steel, LPG cylinders, helmets
, and food products. The ISI mark guarantees quality, safety and reliability of products. Manufacturers must apply to BIS and get their products tested in BIS recognized laboratories.

Steps to apply for BIS certification: 1) Visit the BIS portal manakonline.in and register as a manufacturer. 2) Select the applicable Indian Standard for your product. 3) Fill the application form and upload required documents including factory details, equipment list, and quality control details. 4) Pay the application fee and marking fee. 5) A BI

S officer will visit your factory for inspection. 6) Send product samples to a BIS recognized laboratory for testing. 7) If all tests pass, BIS will issue the license allowing use of ISI mark on your product. The entire process takes approximately 30-90 days depending on product category.

Documents required for BIS certification application include: Manufacturer registration certificate, Factory license, Product details and specifications, Quality control manual, List of testing equipment, Layout plan of factory, Details of key personnel, Previous test reports if any, Trademark registration (if applicable), and Bank details. All doc

BIS Hallmarking is a quality certification of precious metal articles in India. Hallmarking of gold jewellery is mandatory in India since 2023. The BIS hallmark consists of: 1) BIS logo, 2) Purity grade such as 916 for 22 karat gold, 585 for 14 karat gold, 375 for 9 karat gold, 999 for 24 karat gold, 925 for silver, 3) A unique HUID (Hallmark Uniqu
e Identification) number. Consumers can verify hallmark genuineness using the BIS Care mobile app. Jewellers need a BIS licence to sell hallmarked jewellery.

To apply for BIS hallmarking licence: 1) The jewellery must be manufactured by a BIS licensed jeweller. 2) Submit application through BIS portal. 3) The jewellery is tested and hallmarking is done at a BIS recognized hallmarking centre. 4) Each piece gets a unique HUID number. 5) The hallmark includes BIS logo, purity mark, and assaying and hallmar
king centre mark. Hallmarking ensures consumers get the correct purity of gold and silver jewellery they pay for.

BIS formulates Indian Standards through a consultative process involving industry, consumers, scientific institutions and government. There are more than 20000 Indian Standards in force covering various sectors including food, agriculture, chemicals, civil engineering, electrical, electronics, mechanical, metallurgical, medical equipment and textil
es. Standards can be purchased from the BIS website at bis.gov.in or from standardsbis.in portal.

BIS has a network of NABL accredited laboratories to support certification and testing activities. Services include testing of products for certification, calibration services, and training. BIS maintains a list of recognized laboratories across India for product testing. Lab fees and list of recognized labs are available at crsbis.in. Manufacturer
s can get their products tested at any BIS recognized laboratory for certification purposes.

BIS runs consumer awareness programmes to educate people about standardization and quality. Consumers can report fake ISI mark products via BIS Care mobile app, the bis.gov.in portal, or any BIS regional or branch office. BIS conducts raids and takes legal action against manufacturers misusing the ISI mark. BIS organizes consumer connect programmes
across India. Consumers can also check authenticity of BIS certified products online.

To file a complaint with BIS about fake or substandard products: 1) Download BIS Care app from Play Store/App Store. 2) Register your complaint with product details, purchase receipt, and photos. 3) BIS will investigate and take action against the violator. 4) You can also complain via the BIS website or visit nearest BIS office. Complaints are typ

BIS conformity assessment schemes include: 1) Product Certification Scheme with ISI Mark for industrial products. 2) Compulsory Registration Scheme (CRS) for electronics and IT products like mobile phones, laptops, LED lights and power banks. 3) Foreign Manufacturers Certification Scheme (FMCS) for imported products. 4) Management System Certificat
ion for ISO standards. 5) Hallmarking Scheme for gold and silver jewellery. 6) Eco Mark Scheme for environment friendly products.

The Compulsory Registration Scheme (CRS) under BIS makes it mandatory for electronics and IT products to be registered before sale in India. Products covered include: mobile phones, laptops, tablets, LED lights, power banks, smart watches, printers,fax machines, microwave ovens, and electric irons. Foreign manufacturers must appoint an Authorized I
ndian Representative (AIR) to register their products. Registration is valid for 2 years and can be renewed.

The Foreign Manufacturers Certification Scheme (FMCS) allows foreign manufacturers to obtain BIS certification for products exported to India. The foreign manufacturer applies through an Authorized Indian Representative (AIR) based in India. BIS conducts factory inspection abroad either directly or through third party. Products must conform to rele
vant Indian Standards. FMCS ensures imported products meet the same quality standards as domestically manufactured products.

BIS represents India in international and regional standards bodies including ISO (International Organization for Standardization) and IEC (International Electrotechnical Commission). BIS has signed MoUs with national standards bodies of several countries for mutual cooperation. BIS participates in Pacific Area Standards Congress (PASC) and South A
sian Regional Standards Organization (SARSO). International cooperation helps align Indian Standards with global standards and facilitates trade.

BIS is established under the Bureau of Indian Standards Act 2016 which replaced the BIS Act 1986. The Act empowers the government to make certification of any article compulsory. The BIS Conformity Assessment Regulations 2018 govern the certification process. Misuse of BIS certification marks is a punishable offence under the Act with penalties inc
luding imprisonment and fine. BIS can conduct raids and take legal action against violators.

BIS certification fees include: non-refundable application fee (varies by product category), Annual Minimum Marking Fee (AMMF), and marking fee based on production quantity. For ISI certification, fees range from Rs. 5,000 to Rs. 50,000 depending on product type. Testing charges are separate and paid to the testing laboratory. For exact fee details
visit manakonline.in or contact nearest BIS regional or branch office.

Consumers can file complaints against fake BIS certified products via: BIS Care mobile app, consumer portal on bis.gov.in, or any BIS regional or branch office. BIS has regional offices in major cities including Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai, Bangalore, Hyderabad, and Ahmedabad. Enforcement actions include raids, seizures and legal proceedings ag
ainst violators. For grievances related to BIS services, complaints can be filed online at bis.gov.in.

BIS publishes annual reports, Indian Standards, technical guides and consumer awareness materials. Annual reports are available on the BIS website. Standards can be purchased online from bis.gov.in or from the Standards Sale Counter at BIS head office. BIS also publishes handbooks and special publications for various sectors. Press releases and new
s updates are regularly published on the BIS website.

The BIS online portal manakonline.in is the official platform for all certification related activities. Manufacturers can: register online, submit application for product certification, track application status, pay fees online, download license certificates, and manage their profile. The portal also provides list of Indian Standards, recognized la

The ISI Mark is a certification mark issued by BIS for product quality and safety. Products bearing ISI mark have been tested as conforming to Indian Standards. Benefits of ISI mark include: consumer trust and confidence, legal acceptance as proof of quality, competitive advantage in market, and compliance with regulatory requirements. The ISI mark
is one of the most recognized quality marks in India.

BIS has regional offices in major Indian cities: Delhi (North), Mumbai (West), Kolkata (East), Chennai (South), Bangalore, Hyderabad, and Ahmedabad. Additionally, BIS has branch offices in over 30 cities across India. Contact details are available on bis.gov.in. Customers can visit nearest office for certification queries, complaints, and general i
